use fusion_core::{Device, Intent, TaskProfile};
use ndarray::{Array1, Array2};
use std::sync::RwLock;
use tracing::{debug};

/// Represents the "Brain" of the scheduler.
pub struct CortexEngine {
    model: RwLock<Option<SimpleLinearModel>>,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
struct SimpleLinearModel {
    weights: Array2<f32>,
    bias: Array1<f32>,
}

impl CortexEngine {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { model: RwLock::new(None) }
    }

    pub fn schedule(&self, profile: &TaskProfile) -> Device {
        // Feature Engineering
        let features = Array1::from_vec(vec![
            (profile.estimated_ops as f32).log10(),
            (profile.memory_footprint_bytes as f32).log10(),
            profile.intent as i32 as f32,
        ]);

        // Mock Inference (Production would use self.model)
        let gpu_score = features[0] * 0.8 + features[1] * 0.2;
        
        // HFT Guard
        if profile.intent == Intent::Critical {
            return Device::Cpu;
        }

        if gpu_score > 5.0 {
            Device::Gpu(0)
        } else {
            Device::Cpu
        }
    }
}
